channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Bronny James discusses the challenge of dealing with his mental health following cardiac scare
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — The final buzzer sounded Saturday, the South Bay Lakers had won a game and Bronny James seemed completely at ease. Big smile. Handshakes with everyone. Even did a little dance step as he got to the bench.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mahomes throws for TD and runs for score as Chiefs beat Texans 27-19 to close in on No. 1 seed
item.description: Patrick Mahomes threw for 260 yards and a touchdown while running for a score on his ailing ankle, and the Kansas City Chiefs took a big step toward securing the No. 1 seed in the playoffs with a 27-19 victory over the Houston Texans.
item.dc:creator: Dave Skretta, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Penn State opens the College Football Playoff by steamrolling mistake-prone SMU in 38-10 rout
item.description: STATE COLLEGE, Pa. (AP) — Dominic DeLuca and Tony Rojas returned interceptions for touchdowns and Penn State toyed with mistake-prone SMU in a 38-10 victory on Saturday in the opening round of the College Football Playoff.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former San Diego Padres player Rickey Henderson dies at 65: reports
item.description: Rickey Henderson, the man who changed Major League Baseball with his blazing, unbeatable speed, has died, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Knueppel scores 18 points as No. 5 Duke romps to 82-56 win over Georgia Tech
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Kon Knueppel scored 18 points, Cooper Flagg celebrated his 18th birthday by chipping in 13 and No. 5 Duke romped to an 82-56 victory over Georgia Tech on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: PAUL NEWBERRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NBA fines Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla $35,000 for postgame comments toward officials
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Joe Mazzulla's Christmas greetings were costly. The NBA fined the Boston Celtics coach $35,000 on Saturday “for aggressively pursuing and directing inappropriate language toward a game official” at the end of his team's loss to the Chicago Bulls on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Sharks’ Celebrini vs. Oilers’ McDavid would be tantalizing matchup — if it happens
item.description: The San Jose Sharks face the Edmonton Oilers at Rogers Place on Saturday to begin a quick two-game road trip.
item.dc:creator: Curtis Pashelka, The Mercury News

item.title: Notre Dame protects home field in new postseason era with 1st playoff win, 27-17 over Indiana
item.description: SOUTH BEND, Ind. (AP) — Marcus Freeman learned the hard lessons from all those big losses as well as the inexplicable ones.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Guenther scores twice as Utah HC beats Minnesota, 2-1
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club is in seventh heaven right about now. Dylan Guenther scored twice, including the game-winner at 10:01 of the third period, as Utah ran its road winning streak to seven in a row Friday night with a hard-fought 2-1 victory over the Minnesota Wild.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: The Thunder should have been quite tired. They went 2-0 in Florida anyway, pushing record to 22-5
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Here's how the last few days have gone for the Oklahoma City Thunder: Lose the NBA Cup final to Milwaukee in Las Vegas on Tuesday, spend time airborne over four different time zones on Wednesday, win in Orlando on Thursday, win in Miami on Friday.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former NFL star Michael Vick officially hired by Norfolk State on Friday
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Norfolk State officially named Michael Vick its head football coach Friday, the latest hire of a talented NFL player with no college coaching experience to lead an HBCU program.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bronny James is quietly having a solid December in the G League
item.description: Even underneath the shade of his legendary father, the spotlight on LeBron "Bronny" James Jr. was perhaps too much for any young player at the start of his professional basketball career — but months later, it seems as though he's figuring things out his own way.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Mikheyev stars as 'Hawks beat Kraken 3-1 for 3rd straight win
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Ilya Mikheyev scored two more goals, Arvid Soderblom made 27 saves and the Chicago Blackhawks beat the Seattle Kraken 3-1 on Thursday night for their third straight win. Teuvo Teravainen had two assists as Chicago closed out a perfect three-game homestand.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Winter Classic Day 2: Palisade boys show off versatility in big win over Moffat County
item.description: On day two of the annual Winter Classic presented by Family Health West, the Palisade Bulldogs took center stage on their home court.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: San Diego FC releases 2025 regular season schedule
item.description: San Diego FC will be kicking off its first regular season match in franchise history in February against a rival city.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Bay FC Treasure Island training facility lease approved by SF mayor
item.description: (BCN) --San Francisco Mayor London Breed signed legislation on Thursday that will allow the women's professional soccer team Bay Football Club, or Bay FC, to make Treasure Island their future home with a permanent training center.
item.dc:creator: Bay City News, KRON4

item.title: Ducks to honor fellow O.C. team at promotional night
item.description: If you're a fan of Orange County sports, you're in luck this January. On Jan. 7, the Anaheim Ducks are hosting Angels Night, an event that includes a baseball jersey giveaway for the first 5,000 fans in attendance. The game against the Calgary Flames begins at 7 p.m. Tickets begin at less than $30.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5

item.title: Utah HC erases two-goal deficit to stun Vancouver
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Mikhail Sergachev scored with 12 seconds left in overtime, as the Utah Hockey Club stormed back from a 2-0 deficit in the third period to beat the Vanouver Canucks and end a five-game home losing streak, 3-2.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: American competitive eater Joey Chestnut named Holiday Bowl Parade Grand Marshall
item.description: World-famous competitive eater Joey Chestnut has been chosen as the 2024 DIRECTV Holiday Bowl "Chief Eggnog Official." He will also serve as the Grand Marshal of the Holiday Bowl Parade.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Boys Basketball: La Jolla 55, Army-Navy 47
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- Boys hoops in La Jolla with the Vikings hosting Army-Navy from Carlsbad. Great game that went right down to the wire, La Jolla won 52-47. Vikings were lead by Leo Hawkinson with 16. Warriors were lead by Yanler Ramos game high 26 points.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: Government funding deal opens door to move Washington Commanders stadium to DC
item.description: The Washington Commanders could soon be kicking off in the heart of the nation’s capital. The government funding deal unveiled by congressional negotiators Tuesday paved the way for moving the Commanders' stadium from Maryland to Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Gimmicks, not football, dominating college bowl season
item.description: College bowl games are becoming less about football and more about marketing in an ever-changing sports landscape
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bass, NewsNation

item.title: Fruita's Liv Campbell hits 1K point mark as experienced 'Cats are loaded in '24-25
item.description: Coming off a tremendous 19-4 season that saw them earn a top-10 seed in the 6A playoffs, Fruita Monument girls basketball is reloaded this season.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Column: The Chicago Bears have no fire in the trenches
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — When it comes to what's wrong with the Chicago Bears, some argue it's coaching. Others dare to say it's Caleb Williams. Most toss it up to organizational dysfunction, or some supernatural hex inadvertently brought about by the McCaskeys being the McCaskeys.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: BYU excited to face Heisman winner Travis Hunter
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - As BYU continues preparation for the Alamo Bowl December 28th against Colorado, they are looking forward to facing off against Heisman Trophy Winner Travis Hunter. Hunter is truly a one-of-a-kind player.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Isaac Wilson decides to return to Utah
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Upon second thought, Isaac Wilson is returning to Utah. Eight days after announcing he was entering the transfer portal, Wilson announced that he is returning to Utah, where will compete for the starting quarterback job with New Mexico transfer Devon Dampier.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Broncos def. Colts 31-13
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)— Playoff pressure makes diamonds, and the Denver Broncos proved they can shine when it counts, delivering a dominant second-half performance to defeat the Indianapolis Colts 31-13 on Sunday. The game started with postseason hopes hanging in the balance for the Broncos.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Bedard scores in 3rd period as the Blackhawks beat the Islanders 5-3
item.description: Connor Bedard scored with 54 seconds left and Arvid Soderblom made 30 saves, helping the Chicago Blackhawks beat the New York Islanders 5-3 on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Aggies set school record with 10-0 start
item.description: LOGAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Utah State has been playing basketball since 1903. Only four times have the Aggies started a season with nine straight wins. On Saturday, this Utah State team became the first to win ten in a row to start a season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
